# 0006. Generated File Safety Boundary

## Status

Accepted

## Context

Architect Companion writes files into consuming repositories. Those files often
share names with conventional hand-written documents — `AGENTS.md`,
`.cursor/rules/*.mdc`, CI workflows — and the same names already exist in many
repositories that would adopt the tool.

The renderer must be safe to run repeatedly, including the first time it runs
in a repository that already contains a file at one of its output paths. It
must also be safe to compose with other tools or symlinks that share a target
location.

Two related questions follow from this:

- Should generated files support managed sections inside otherwise hand-written
  files, or should the renderer own whole files?
- How does the renderer recognize a file as its own before overwriting it?

## Decision

Generated files are owned end-to-end by Architect Companion, and every
rendered output carries an explicit generated-file marker.

Concretely, the render pipeline enforces these invariants:

- Each renderer produces a complete file, not a managed region inside another
  file.
- Rendered content must include the marker string
  `Generated by Architect Companion. Edit .architect-companion/ instead.`
  near the top of the file. Output missing the marker is a render error.
- Existing files at a render target are overwritten only when their current
  content already contains the marker. Unmarked files are never overwritten;
  the renderer fails with a clear message instead.
- Output paths must resolve inside the project directory. Any segment of the
  resolved path that is a symbolic link causes the renderer to refuse to
  write.
- `render --check` reports the same staleness whether a target is missing or
  whose marked content differs from what would be rendered, and exits non-zero.

## Consequences

- Adoption in an existing repository is safe by default. A user who already
  has an `AGENTS.md` or workflow file at the same path gets a refusal, not a
  silent overwrite.
- Renderer authors do not have to reason about merging into existing user
  content. The simplest correct renderer is a full-file generator.
- Removing the marker from a generated file opts that file out of further
  management. This is intentional: it gives users an escape hatch when they
  want to take over a file, at the cost of future regeneration.
- Managed-section support inside hand-written files is not foreclosed, but it
  is out of scope until a concrete target requires it. Adding it later means
  introducing a second ownership mode, not changing the default.
- Golden-file tests must include the marker; CI catches accidental marker
  removal.
- The symlink and path-escape checks protect against rendering through links
  that point outside the project. They add a small filesystem cost per render
  but eliminate a class of footguns.
